post.description:
Retrieve games with flexible filtering and attribute selection.

This endpoint supports:
- Filtering by game IDs, search term, orientation, tags
- Selective attribute loading (tags, platforms, matches, screenshots, etc.)
- Pagination with offset and limit
- Returns base fields by default, additional attributes on request

Retrieve Games Including Matches.description: Get games with their related match configurations

post.description: Creates or updates player data key-value pairs for the authenticated user. Each key can store a JSON object with custom data. This is useful for storing player preferences, game progress, settings, and any custom metadata.

requestBody.description: Player data to update with multiple examples

singleKey.description: Creates or updates a single data key with settings

multipleKeys.description: Updates multiple data keys at once

withPermission.description: Updates data with public permission (visible to other players)

multipleKeys.description: Updates both rewards and flags keys for the specified player

post.description: Removes specified data keys from a player's data store. This permanently deletes the keys and their values. Useful for cleaning up deprecated data or removing specific player data fields.

requestBody.description: Keys to remove from player data

singleKey.description: Removes the tempSettings key from player-123's data

multipleKeys.description: Removes all three specified keys from the player's data

Gets a new access token using the entity token and expiring access token. Use this endpoint to get a new access token before the current one expires. The entity token is a long-lived token provided during login/signup and can be reused for multiple refreshes.

refreshToken.description: Exchange entity token and expiring access token for a new access token

post.description: Verifies if an access token is still valid and returns user information extracted from the token. Can be used by game servers to validate player tokens. Token validation doesn't consume or modify the token.

validateWithEntity.description: Validate access token with additional entity token verification

info.title: Specter API - Gaming Backend-as-a-Service
info.description:
What is Specter?

Specter is a Backend-as-a-Service (BaaS) platform designed specifically for game developers. It provides scalable, real-time backend solutions for games and gamification projects.

Key Features

  • Player Management - Unified player profiles, cross-platform authentication
  • Economy & Inventory - Multi-currency systems, items, bundles, dynamic storefronts
  • Achievements & Missions - Tasks, battle passes, rewards, skill divisions
  • LiveOps - Remote content scheduling, campaigns without new game builds
  • Competitions & Leaderboards - Tournament engine, bracket formats, prize payouts
  • Social & Community - Referrals, friends systems, real-time announcements

Getting Started

  1. Sign up at https://specterapp.xyz
  2. Create a project in the dashboard
  3. Get your API key from Project Settings
  4. Integrate using this REST API with your game engine (Unity, Unreal, etc.)

Authentication

All API calls require:

  • Api-Key header - Your project API key
  • Bearer Token - Player authentication token (for player-specific endpoints)

Platform Support

Unity, Unreal Engine, PlayStation, Xbox, Nintendo, Steam, iOS, Android

Resources

  • 📥 OpenAPI JSON Spec
  • 🌐 Website
